110557.explanation: Residents must be able to express their opinions even after the elections. This can be done in various ways, including a referendum. We find involving residents important and consider per topic what fits best. In Beverwijk it is established how a referendum can be organized.

110563.explanation: Many labor migrants in Beverwijk work in sectors other than agriculture and horticulture. They are important to the economy, but housing is often poorly arranged and too concentrated. Therefore, we tackle illegal rentals and focus on regional housing and housing on private property.

110564.explanation: Tree felling must be limited to a minimum. If felling is necessary, there must always be full compensation, with at least one new tree planted. This way, we preserve existing greenery. Additionally, we focus on further greening with more trees and green spaces for a healthy and livable municipality.

110568.explanation: Bulk waste processing is paid for through the waste disposal levy. Pickup at home can remain free on fixed collection days. This keeps costs manageable and prevents dumping.

110571.explanation: If a farmer stops, we consider it desirable that existing buildings can be converted to housing. This hardly applies in Beverwijk because there are few agricultural businesses. Farms are mainly located in the Wijkermeerpolder, which we want to keep open.

110572.explanation: People on welfare can receive reimbursements via the Youth Fund and Adults Fund for Sports & Culture. The municipality does not have to pay the membership fee in full but can provide support so that sports and thus a healthy and social life are possible for everyone.

110573.explanation: A strong local broadcaster is essential for a well-informed society. Extra support can be structural or one-time, provided it contributes to independent and professional local journalism. This strengthens the regional broadcaster in the IJmond and local democracy.

110576.explanation: Associations are primarily responsible for their own finances. Structural financial support from the municipality is not a given. The municipality does offer support in other ways, for example through the sports desk. In certain situations, (temporary) financial aid may be necessary and appropriate.

110578.explanation: More visible and approachable enforcement officers (BOAs), especially with extra efforts in the evenings, contribute to a safer Beverwijk. They play an important role in prevention, supervision, and quickly addressing nuisance. Extra focus on enforcement increases livability and the sense of safety.

110579.explanation: Camera surveillance can contribute to safety in Beverwijk. We deploy it targeted and temporarily in places where it is needed, depending on the safety issue. Camera surveillance must always be proportional, effectively contribute to safety, and comply with clear privacy rules.

110581.explanation: We continue the starter's loan. This scheme is often indispensable for people who want to buy their first home. This gives starters more room to finance a house. Preferably, we give priority to Beverwijk residents so they have more opportunities within their own municipality.

110583.explanation: Wijk aan Zee is Beverwijk’s village. We do not rule out housing construction outside the built-up area in advance. Under certain conditions, we see opportunities for housing to keep the village vital. This is complicated because the province decides and strict rules apply due to Natura 2000 and the National Ecological Network (NNN).

110584.explanation: People on welfare must be actively encouraged to participate in society. Meaningful social work can help gain rhythm and experience but should not be compulsory. Tailored solutions and personal guidance remain important.

110585.explanation: Exemption from municipal taxes is important for residents with low incomes. This prevents people with financial problems from falling deeper into difficulties. The municipality must ensure security of existence and tailor-made solutions for those who need it.

110586.explanation: The income threshold to receive help with poverty must keep pace with inflation. This prevents residents from falling through the cracks due to rising prices and ensures aid remains accessible for those who need it.

110588.explanation: Electric driving is growing, and charging points are needed. At the same time, parking pressure is high, and many residents find charging points inconvenient. Therefore, we expand the number of charging points carefully and based on need, without unnecessarily eliminating parking spaces.

110589.explanation: Within the built-up area and in the center, 30 km/h becomes the norm. Experience, including in our own municipality, sadly shows that lower speeds save lives. At 30 km/h, the number of traffic victims demonstrably decreases. 50 km/h remains on main arterial roads, such as the Plesmanweg.

110590.explanation: Good care must be respectful and appropriate, with attention to a person’s background. At the same time, care must be available to everyone and of good quality. Therefore, tailored care is important, without 'always being able to choose' becoming a hard requirement.

110592.explanation: These revenues are necessary to continue providing facilities and municipal tasks properly. We do not increase property tax (OZB), except for inflation correction, unless good facilities and the development of our municipality are at risk.

110595.explanation: Beverwijk is a compact municipality with little room to expand. Our priority is housing construction and a healthy living environment. Therefore, we do not opt for new business parks but for better utilization of existing ones. This creates space without extra burden on the surroundings.

110597.explanation: Everyone should have the same chance at social rental housing. Refugees with a residence permit therefore find housing themselves, just like others. Priority can only be given in special situations, such as medical urgency.

110600.explanation: The current Sportfondsenbad is outdated and does not meet modern requirements. We will build a new, modern, affordable, and accessible swimming pool in Beverwijk, preferably combined with a sports hall. This enables everyone to exercise, be active, and obtain a swimming diploma.

110604.explanation: New establishments are not needed and undesirable. In the recent period, we have gone from four to two coffeeshops, which is sufficient for the municipality and its surroundings.

110605.explanation: Social rental housing is important and must be sufficiently available. At the same time, Beverwijk already has about 36% social rental housing. Therefore, we apply neighborhood differentiation: on average, we aim for at least 30%, but per neighborhood and project, we consider what is needed for a good and balanced housing mix.

110607.explanation: Renting out individual rooms leads to too much nuisance and problems, especially in the center and in Wijk aan Zee. Room rental must therefore be reduced and only allowed under strict conditions, with clear rules and enforcement to protect livability.

110609.explanation: In many neighborhoods, parking pressure is high. Additional parking spaces may be necessary and sometimes can be realized in a smart way. We do this in consultation with residents and with attention to livability and greenery.

110611.explanation: We do not support DIFTAR (paying per garbage bag) and focus on separating residual waste after collection.

110612.explanation: When financially feasible, we want to make it possible to park free for the first two hours. In any case, we maintain the recently introduced first hour free parking, a clear improvement compared to before that visitors appreciate and that makes the center more attractive.

110613.explanation: A referendum is only necessary if an actual merger with Heemskerk or Velsen is being prepared. In that case, it must be submitted to the residents of Beverwijk so they can express their opinion on such a drastic decision.

110614.explanation: Camera surveillance can contribute to safety in the Kuenenkwartier and on Europaplein. We deploy cameras only temporarily where necessary, targeted and proportionate. Clear privacy rules apply, and surveillance must demonstrably contribute to a safer environment.

110557.explanation: As residents, we govern our own municipality. When an issue plays a major role, a referendum can be a fair and effective way to make a decision. That way, we involve people in local democracy.

110563.explanation: We want to put an end to buying up houses to accommodate foreign (seasonal) workers. We also aim to enforce owner-occupancy where needed (at least in Wijk aan Zee).

110564.explanation: Trees are important for livability. Think of the lower temperature in the shade of a tree. Beverwijk is a municipality with few trees, so we continue the existing tree policy and add more trees.

110568.explanation: Garbage, old mattresses, and other litter on the street are annoying, unsafe, and unhealthy. The easiest way to prevent this is by collecting bulky waste for free. This avoids hassle and gives us cleaner streets.

110571.explanation: This is possible within the existing frameworks of protected landscapes. Because the entire outer area of Beverwijk is protected landscape, this is practically not or hardly possible.

110572.explanation: Sports give a lot of energy and satisfaction. And it keeps you healthy. But for people with welfare benefits, sports are often too expensive. We believe the municipality should ensure this group can participate in a sports club for free.

110573.explanation: Local media are extremely important to know what is happening in your own area, from the street to the municipal council. But many local media struggle to keep afloat. We believe the municipality should contribute to this.

110576.explanation: Sports provide lots of energy and satisfaction. They keep you healthy, and often the sports club is also a meeting place. If a sports club collapses, an entire neighborhood suffers, so we believe the municipality should intervene if necessary.

110578.explanation: It is very important to feel safe on the street. That is why we choose more enforcement officers on the street who can intervene immediately if something goes wrong.

110579.explanation: Feeling safe on the street is very important. But more cameras are not the right solution in our opinion. Your privacy is a great asset that should not be violated lightly. Therefore, we prefer more street enforcement officers who can intervene immediately if something goes wrong.

110581.explanation: The Beverwijk starter loan, like the sustainability loan, is a great success. It gives young people a bit more financial leeway, enabling them to buy their own home.

110583.explanation: Given that outside the built-up area there is mainly protected nature, the possibilities are very limited. We want to preserve and protect the green areas around our town as well as possible. But to tackle the housing shortage, we also need to use agricultural land for housing.

110584.explanation: People are not on welfare because they don’t want to work. They need a stable foundation, affordable housing, and trust from the municipality. Extra demands and conditions only create more uncertainty and cause new problems.

110585.explanation: If the exemption of municipal taxes stops, people with the lowest incomes in our municipality will get into serious trouble. They simply cannot spare that money. We believe everyone should be able to keep their head above water; therefore, we support tax exemption.

110586.explanation: The municipality could provide support if income is at 120% of the minimum level. After all, the minimum social assistance level is insufficient to live without poverty and without support from food and clothing banks.

110588.explanation: The municipality must keep the number of charging points for electric cars in line with the number of electric cars. This can also be done by better supporting home charging, for example by permitting cable mats.

110589.explanation: The figures clearly show: a lower maximum speed makes traffic in our municipality much safer. For cycling children and elderly people with reduced mobility, this makes a huge difference. Moreover, the streets become quieter and the air cleaner.

110590.explanation: For both parties, care recipient and care provider, this is the best option. This also ensures a good and safe environment in which care is provided.

110592.explanation: The property tax (OZB) in Beverwijk is already low compared to surrounding municipalities. This income is essential to pay for our facilities: roads, sports, culture. A discussion should therefore be about what we would want to cut.

110595.explanation: There is no more space for new business parks. The space is simply used up. Therefore, the only available space is in existing business parks.

110597.explanation: Our municipality implements national legislation. As long as this has not been amended, it is always leading.

110600.explanation: A swimming pool is important in a coastal municipality. The current pool is technically outdated. Therefore, building a new swimming pool is necessary. Preferably, this is done in cooperation with Heemskerk.

110604.explanation: Beverwijk already has a regional role regarding the number of coffeeshops. The current number is more than sufficient.

110605.explanation: There are long waiting lists for social rental homes. Therefore, sufficient new building is essential. Beverwijk already has a substantial amount of social rental homes, which should definitely not decrease. 30% is the existing standard in Beverwijk for new constructions, and we will maintain that.

110607.explanation: We have a huge housing shortage, and many people in our municipality live more spaciously than necessary. Therefore, we want to make it easier to rent out individual rooms, provided they are well maintained and charged at a fair rental price. We prevent abuses through a permit system.

110609.explanation: When restructuring existing neighborhoods and redesigning roads, an optimal balance between parking spaces and greenery is always sought.

110611.explanation: The municipality is one of the first in the Netherlands to introduce this on a voluntary basis from 2027. Residents will receive a discount if they deliver fewer waste bags or bins.

110612.explanation: Parking fees are intended to regulate the use of scarce parking spaces. Whether extending free parking from 1 to 2 hours and charging a higher rate after 2 hours will contribute to this needs further investigation.

110613.explanation: Two or three IJmond municipalities together have more clout and can more easily obtain national subsidies. As residents, we decide over our own municipality. A major issue such as a merger can be a good topic for a referendum.

110614.explanation: It is very important to feel safe on the street. But we do not consider permanent cameras the right solution. Your privacy is a great asset that must not be infringed lightly. That is why we prefer more enforcement officers on the street who can intervene immediately if something goes wrong.

110557.explanation: D66 believes a referendum gives residents real influence. It strengthens local democracy. But not all subjects are suitable for a yes-or-no vote, so we carefully consider when it is appropriate. Referenda may increase trust in politics.

110563.explanation: Labor migrants are indispensable for our economy. D66 believes housing should be part of a broader housing and labor market plan. We support humane housing; therefore, the conversion and splitting up of existing homes must be actively combated in the current housing stock.

110564.explanation: Beverwijk already implements an ambitious tree policy: for every tree cut down, at least one new tree, preferably more, to make the city greener and cooler. These trees must indeed return to our city or village.

110568.explanation: Bulky waste must be collected for free by appointment in Beverwijk, for a cleaner city and more convenience for residents.

110571.explanation: Space is scarce in the Netherlands. But as far as D66 is concerned, there is no more room for too many taboos in solving the housing crisis. We will continue to carefully consider each specific location. But if we keep doing what we did, we will maintain the low building figures.

110572.explanation: D66 supports the current policy to reimburse less affluent residents for sports club fees and sports equipment. Sports are healthy for everyone.

110573.explanation: D66 supports the merging of local broadcasters into a regional broadcaster. This will be funded by the national government, ensuring independence.

110576.explanation: D66 supports the municipal efforts to aid associations with a youth sports subsidy. In addition, the municipality participates in the Youth Fund for Sports & Culture.

110578.explanation: D66 believes everyone should live in a pleasant and safe neighborhood. Maintaining supervision also contributes to this. However, BOAs (special enforcement officers) cannot be solely responsible for the safety of our residents. This must be addressed with a mix of preventive and repressive measures.

110579.explanation: D66 believes that camera surveillance, as one of a mix of measures, can be used in places with serious nuisance and public order disturbances. This must of course be proportionate and compliant with privacy laws.

110581.explanation: D66 believes that first-time homebuyers should have a good chance to enter the housing market; the municipality already offers the starter loan for this purpose. We fully support this.

110583.explanation: D66 favors building on the borders of the municipality alongside densifying existing areas. However, we want to protect the rural area.

110584.explanation: D66 is cautious about making reciprocity obligations mandatory for people receiving social assistance. It is mainly about tailored solutions, trust, and guidance to employment. However, we do encourage meaningful volunteer activities.

110585.explanation: D66 remains a supporter of financially assisting residents who find it difficult. The exemption scheme for municipal taxes is part of this.

110586.explanation: D66 supports financially assisting residents who are struggling. While support should primarily be tailored, an income threshold is one of the criteria.

110588.explanation: D66 supports the regulated provision of charging points in public spaces. Additionally, we encourage home charging by allowing charging cables across sidewalks.

110589.explanation: D66 advocates lowering the maximum speed in residential neighborhoods to 30 km/h for the safety of children, pedestrians, and cyclists.

110590.explanation: D66 supports free choice of healthcare but not unlimited. There must be a balance between patient freedom of choice and the affordability and quality of care.

110592.explanation: For years, D66 has aimed to only index the property tax (OZB) minimally. This way, we remain well below the national average.

110595.explanation: Beverwijk has several spacious business parks, including one of the three parks with environmental class 5 in North Holland. D66 continues to invest to make our business parks more sustainable and safe.

110597.explanation: In Beverwijk, status holders do not automatically get priority in the allocation of social rental housing unless they have an urgent need.

110600.explanation: D66 wants to build a new sustainable swimming pool as soon as possible to replace the current one. A facility that, besides swimming lessons and recreational swimming, also supports swim clubs.

110604.explanation: The coffeeshops in Beverwijk currently serve a regional function. D66 believes the municipality should cooperate with surrounding municipalities to achieve better distribution of coffeeshops. This means we advocate for a number of coffeeshops that match the demand.

110605.explanation: At present, 36% of all homes in Beverwijk are social rental. In new construction projects, a portion is social rental so that we grow towards a share of 30%.

110607.explanation: D66 supports subletting (hospitahousing), where the resident rents out a room they have available. However, renting out multiple rooms while the owner does not live there is opposed by D66.

110609.explanation: For D66, there must of course be parking spaces in residential neighborhoods, but there should also be sufficient space for greenery and recreation.

110611.explanation: D66 supports the new raw materials policy whereby each resident can choose to receive a discount on the waste tax by limiting the amount of waste they produce.

110612.explanation: Beverwijk already has hospitable parking, meaning that the first hour of parking in the center is free. Visitors usually stay briefly, so the first hour free is an attractive and affordable option.

110613.explanation: D66 favors more collaboration in the IJmond region. We are also open to a merger. Depending on the question, this could be studied via a referendum.

110614.explanation: D66 is not opposed to camera surveillance. However, it is important that it is used proportionally and effectively for a certain period. Privacy aspects must also be carefully considered.

110557.explanation: The mandate of the people's representative should suffice. Only in exceptional cases should a referendum be considered, but it is not preferred.

110563.explanation: This is very dependent on the quality of housing on the individual property.

110564.explanation: We support a good and well-thought-out green and maintenance plan for the entire municipality. In our view, quality takes precedence over quantity.

110568.explanation: We want to keep the waste disposal tax as low as possible for our residents, so we agree with the current system, which includes a substantial free portion.

110571.explanation: Zoning plans are leading but can be adjusted. The municipality can collaborate with landowners and explore possibilities.

110572.explanation: There are already national subsidies available via the Sports & Culture Fund. The municipality can play a supportive role in the application process.

110573.explanation: The current arrangements seem sufficient to us.

110576.explanation: Sports clubs play an important social role and are very important for the health of our residents.

110578.explanation: The safety of our residents must be a priority in any case.

110579.explanation: Deploy where needed; this can also be temporary.

110581.explanation: This has already been initiated successfully within the municipality by the VVD. It must continue; every starter deserves a chance in the housing market.

110583.explanation: Where possible, we should stimulate plans for housing construction.

110584.explanation: Where possible, we expect everyone to contribute their share within society.

110585.explanation: Allowances and arrangements are available for this.

110586.explanation: There is a national indexation in this area.

110588.explanation: Primarily, a good policy plan must be drawn up.

110589.explanation: 50 km/h where possible, 30 km/h where necessary.

110590.explanation: This would only increase pressure on healthcare. Good care is fundamental.

110592.explanation: The goal is to keep costs for our residents as low as possible. Where possible, this may involve a reduction.

110595.explanation: There are already sufficient business parks within the municipality of Beverwijk where there is still room for development in the field of our business activities.

110597.explanation: Our residents have just as much right to a home.

110600.explanation: A completely new swimming pool needs to be built in Beverwijk. Renovating the existing pool is not an option.

110604.explanation: There are currently more than enough coffee shops in the municipality of Beverwijk.

110605.explanation: The municipality of Beverwijk is already far above the national average in terms of social housing. As far as VVD Beverwijk is concerned, this is not necessary.

110609.explanation: Through smart solutions and good policies, it may still be possible to create additional spaces.

110611.explanation: The VVD is against Diftar (pay per garbage bag).

110612.explanation: This would be a welcome addition to the current policy.

110613.explanation: At this moment, a study into the desirability is a first step.

110557.explanation: Residents elect the municipal council every four years and thereby give the council the mandate to make decisions for the city. A referendum is only possible on exceptional, weighty issues and at the initiative of residents.

110563.explanation: Housing labor migrants sometimes leads to poor conditions and nuisance. Moreover, Beverwijk and Wijk aan Zee have a relatively large number of labor migrants. We want to address rooming-in through a residential ordinance. This is separate from whether living on the entrepreneur's own premises should be allowed.

110564.explanation: Beverwijk has had a tree balance for years. We must be realistic about our space and tree planting. Vrij! chooses to primarily strengthen Beverwijk's green structure in our parks. We want to expand the parks we have with additional greenery and preserve the Wronki Forest.

110568.explanation: Vrij! supports introducing a fixed monthly collection day for bulky waste. From the neighborhood approach, it appears that many people neatly offer their bulky waste if they are allowed to place it without notification on a certain day. This way, residents don’t have to make an appointment via customer service, which often takes a long time.

110571.explanation: There is a housing shortage, and limited additional housing on one's own property must therefore be possible. Homes on own property must comply with the Decree on Environmental Construction Works (Bbl). Vrij! supports allowing pre-caregiving homes and multi-generational homes.

110572.explanation: Beverwijk has extensive minimum income policies, including a sports pass and reimbursement of veterinary costs. Vrij! believes this support should not only be available to people on social assistance but especially also to working people with low incomes. This ensures everyone can participate.

110573.explanation: Independent local media are of great importance. IJmond360 was recently designated as a regional broadcaster. We provide financial support where necessary, but this must not influence reporting; independence is paramount.

110576.explanation: The municipality is responsible for the sports fields and clubs for the canteen buildings and changing rooms. In principle, it is not the municipality's task to provide financial aid, but if sports clubs, for example due to energy bills, really get into trouble, we will help.

110578.explanation: Beverwijk has many enforcers compared to surrounding municipalities, but their deployment can be more effective. Enforcement should focus on nuisance and safety risks, not on parking fines. We retrain parking enforcement officers into full-fledged enforcers. We also maintain atmosphere management in the city center.

110579.explanation: Camera surveillance is only useful if the police also have time to review the footage. In practice, we see that more visible presence often is more effective than camera surveillance. Where camera surveillance works, we keep it, but we also often choose atmosphere management.

110581.explanation: Beverwijk already has a successful starter loan that helps many young people with their first step in the housing market. We will of course maintain this.

110583.explanation: Vrij! is generally in favor of the 'street next door' approach. However, we see limited possibilities to further expand Beverwijk and Wijk aan Zee beyond the existing boundaries. Concrete proposals will be assessed carefully.

110584.explanation: Vrij! thinks it is important that social assistance recipients who are able to do so remain active in society and preferably return to work as soon as possible. Vrij! supports asking for a counter-performance in the form of volunteer work, which helps maintain work rhythm and builds a network.

110585.explanation: More and more residents are struggling. Not only social assistance recipients, but especially working people and self-employed with low incomes. For these people, we maintain debt remission so they can stay financially afloat.

110586.explanation: Beverwijk applies a norm of 110% of the social assistance threshold to qualify for minimum schemes. We will keep this as it is.

110588.explanation: Residents who buy an electric car can apply for a charging spot under existing regulations. This will remain so. We will not take more regular parking spaces than necessary from the already limited supply.

110589.explanation: Vrij! wants to promote traffic flow and is against downgrading the city center ring and through roads to 30 km/h. Such measures cause congestion, shortcut traffic through residential areas, and actually more unsafe situations.

110590.explanation: Vrij! believes it is important that the quality of care is good and that care services are affordable and measurable. We will make no exceptions based on religion or culture.

110592.explanation: Vrij! believes it is important that Beverwijk maintains the lowest housing costs in the IJmond region. Where possible, we will reduce property tax (OZB).

110595.explanation: Beverwijk has several valuable business parks. We will maintain these areas and, if possible, give existing companies room to expand their operations.

110597.explanation: Vrij! believes that status holders should not be given priority in the social rental housing market, where there are long waiting lists for Beverwijk youth and urgent housing seekers. To meet our legal obligations, we explore other housing forms, such as temporary housing.

110600.explanation: Vrij! firmly supports maintaining a swimming pool in Beverwijk. Our city deserves a complete, accessible swimming facility. Vrij! is strongly against a shared swimming pool in Heemskerk. That 26 million should be invested in a Beverwijks swimming pool.

110604.explanation: Beverwijk plays a regional role when it comes to coffee shops. Vrij! chooses to maintain the current number. It is important that owners actively manage the atmosphere in the neighborhood so their visitors do not cause nuisance. We will not add any coffee shops.

110605.explanation: Beverwijk already has more social rental homes (36%) than average in the region. Vrij! wants to focus mainly on affordable owner-occupied homes in new construction to promote housing flow. We also encourage new housing forms such as pre-caregiving homes, multi-generational homes, and senior cohousing.

110607.explanation: In Beverwijk and Wijk aan Zee, many labor migrants live in roomed housing, often leading to overcrowding, poor living conditions, and nuisance. Vrij! wants to limit rooming-in through a residential ordinance so homes remain on the regular market, and to take strict action against abuses.

110609.explanation: In Beverwijk and Wijk aan Zee, parking pressure is high. Residents often cannot park in their own street, creating a waterbed effect in surrounding streets. Where possible, we add parking spaces and only build new homes with realistic parking norms.

110611.explanation: Vrij! is a strong opponent of DIFTAR (pay per bag). We believe the number of illegal dumping incidents will only increase because properly disposing of waste costs money in this system. Additionally, it contradicts our goal to make waste collection as easy as possible for our residents.

110612.explanation: Certainly, and it doesn’t stop at the first two hours of free parking. Vrij! chooses a welcoming city center area for motorists. We strive for completely free parking in large parking lots. We will bring back the blue zone so locals can again park in their own streets.

110613.explanation: A merger with Heemskerk and Velsen is not on the table. Vrij! wants to shape cooperation with other municipalities based on shared interests from which Beverwijk also benefits. We also look at other municipalities, such as Zaanstad.

110614.explanation: Europaplein is largely located in Heemskerk. Camera surveillance only has added value if the police also have time to review the footage. Often, visible presence of enforcement or atmosphere management is more effective. That is what we primarily focus on.

110557.explanation: In elections, people can choose representatives they trust to make decisions on their behalf. Referenda reduce complex decisions to a yes or no choice, which does not do justice to the complexity of many proposals. Referenda increase divisions in society.

110563.explanation: Companies are responsible for decent housing for their labor migrants.

110564.explanation: As CDA Beverwijk - Wijk aan Zee, we consider greenery in the municipality important. When a tree is cut down, a tree must be planted nearby. If this is not possible, the tree must be planted elsewhere in the municipality.

110568.explanation: This already happens in Beverwijk and we want to maintain it. It is an extra service to the people of Beverwijk and prevents illegal dumping.

110571.explanation: To address the housing shortage, we believe the municipality should be open to these initiatives but should set clear conditions. It is not intended for new residential neighborhoods to arise from this.

110572.explanation: Currently, low-income individuals receive money for sports if they want. As CDA Beverwijk - Wijk aan Zee, we find it important that people continue to participate in society. This scheme contributes to that.

110573.explanation: At this moment, we do not see a need to support the local broadcaster with additional funds. Local broadcasters primarily receive money from the national government and through advertisements. If the local broadcaster comes up with good plans that require extra funding, we will of course consider those plans.

110576.explanation: The municipality can support sports clubs in various ways, for example by guaranteeing loans, but we find direct financial support to be excessive.

110578.explanation: We agree with this. At the same time, there should be more police on the streets. They have more authority.

110579.explanation: For the safety feeling of the people of Beverwijk and the chance to catch criminals, it is important to place more cameras where possible. We are also in favor of cameras for traffic.

110581.explanation: In the municipality of Beverwijk, starters can receive a starter loan for the purchase of their first home. As CDA Beverwijk - Wijk aan Zee, we want to maintain this and continue to top up the starter loan fund with additional money when needed.

110583.explanation: We focus on construction projects within the built-up area, such as Spoorzone. We find it important to consider that Beverwijk has relatively little green open space. Therefore, we do not want to build it all up.

110584.explanation: If people are able, we find it reasonable to ask them to contribute to society through socially meaningful work. This also ensures that people build a network and can find a job faster.

110585.explanation: Low-income individuals are often dependent on these exemption schemes.

110586.explanation: As CDA Beverwijk - Wijk aan Zee, we believe that generous minimum income schemes are applied in Beverwijk. However, we must continue to closely monitor how people just outside these schemes are doing and whether that leads to adjustments.

110588.explanation: Given the (expected) growth in the number of electric cars, the number of charging points should gradually increase.

110589.explanation: We are not in favor of a rigid measure to make the entire built-up area 30 km/h. We want to assess each case to see if 30 km/h demonstrably contributes to traffic safety and fits the road design.

110590.explanation: We find it important that people can make this choice.

110592.explanation: In Beverwijk, our housing costs are below the national average. We, as CDA Beverwijk - Wijk aan Zee, have advocated this for years. To continue investing in our facilities, we want to maintain the property tax as it is.

110595.explanation: We believe there is currently a good balance between housing and business parks with the current plans (Spoorzone).

110597.explanation: Currently, municipalities are still required to house status holders. We will continue to comply with this legal obligation. Giving them priority ensures the asylum process does not get clogged. To maintain support, more homes must be built for other housing seekers.

110600.explanation: We find it important that there remains a good swimming facility in Beverwijk. Renewing the very outdated Sportfondsen swimming pool costs too much money. Therefore, we would like to build a new pool with modern facilities for Beverwijk.

110604.explanation: Coffeeshops are a source of social problems. As far as CDA Beverwijk - Wijk aan Zee is concerned, we try to prevent the establishment of new coffeeshops.

110605.explanation: Beverwijk already has a relatively high number (36%) of social rental homes. As long as the number of social rental homes remains above 30%, CDA Beverwijk - Wijk aan Zee does not find it necessary to require at least 30% social rental in new housing.

110607.explanation: Renting out individual rooms encourages rental to labor migrants. This causes neighborhoods to lose social cohesion and leads to nuisance.

110609.explanation: By better organizing the existing public space, especially during redesigns, we believe more parking spaces can be created in the neighborhoods.

110611.explanation: Paying per garbage bag often seems to correlate with an increase in illegal dumping. We are therefore not in favor of this system. We do keep an eye on the experiences of surrounding municipalities with this system.

110612.explanation: We believe the current parking policy (first hour free) makes the shopping area attractive. As CDA Beverwijk - Wijk aan Zee, we choose to use the financial resources that this measure would cost for a parking garage and housing in the center instead.

110613.explanation: In elections, people can choose representatives they trust to make decisions on their behalf. Referenda reduce complex decisions to a yes or no choice, which does not do justice to the complexity of many proposals. Referenda increase divisions in society.

110614.explanation: To increase the sense of safety and the chance to catch criminals, more camera surveillance must be implemented.

110557.explanation: Yes, we are strong supporters of the binding referendum. That means citizens can directly express their views on important political decisions and the municipality is also obliged to respect the result.

110563.explanation: In the Netherlands, the housing requirements are such that this is hardly possible, but it would solve many problems. Given the pressure on our housing market, we wholeheartedly support this thinking.

110564.explanation: FVD Beverwijk supports fewer rules. We prefer to focus on good policies in spatial planning, nature, and recreation. Not planting trees because you must, but because you can.

110568.explanation: Our waste processing partner HVC offers every household in Beverwijk and Wijk aan Zee the possibility to have bulky waste collected by appointment free of charge. FVD wants to maintain this for a clean municipality and to give residents without their own transport the option to properly dispose of bulky waste.

110571.explanation: The zoning plan and environmental vision are leading for us in this. Agricultural land should remain agricultural land. But if the designation is 'Houses', then the farmer is free to build an additional (care) home.

110572.explanation: The positive effects of sports are twofold. Physical activity leads to better fitness, health, and vitality. This can be achieved in many ways for free. The social aspect of sports clubs is also important but is by no means the municipality's responsibility to fund.

110573.explanation: The newly formed regional broadcaster IJmond360 receives a relatively low municipal contribution compared to other municipalities. If they can convince with sharp, independent reporting, we are open to granting extra funding.

110576.explanation: This is too general a statement. Many factors play a role, including how the club is managed, interest in the sport, how many clubs offer this sport, etc. In specific cases, the municipality could financially contribute.

110578.explanation: First, existing public order officers (BOAs) should focus more on safety rather than issuing parking fines. Furthermore, we will see if extra efforts are needed to give attention neighborhoods a safety boost. Preferably in the form of more police, as they have more authority.

110579.explanation: The entire city is covered with cameras and nothing is achieved. Problems only shift further out of sight. Before you know it, you are in an Orwellian nightmare.

110581.explanation: Beverwijk invests millions in so-called starter loans, which are used frequently. In the bigger picture, this only increases capital on the buyers' side, causing house prices to inevitably rise according to the known supply-and-demand model.

110583.explanation: We believe that all options must be kept open in advance regarding building.

110584.explanation: We consider it desirable that people receiving social assistance benefits provide a certain level of social contribution. This helps them stay connected to society and the labor market. Furthermore, they will appreciate the financial support they receive. And society gets something back for their support.

110585.explanation: For the truly needy, this is a welcome and familiar boost. The municipality is set up for this and can bear the responsibility. It makes no sense to potentially push low-income people into debt by abolishing this scheme.

110586.explanation: This threshold should be adjusted according to inflation. What was a reasonable income 20 years ago is now poverty. Beyond that, we see no reason for expansion.

110588.explanation: There are enough spots for electric cars. Often these are empty while regular cars cannot find parking. Let's first create more space for the old faithful combustion engine vehicles.

110589.explanation: The municipality openly wants to downgrade almost all roads in Beverwijk to 30 km/h, starting with Alkmaarseweg. We are strongly opposed to this. We want to keep driving 50 km/h as much as possible. No Amsterdam-style scenarios in Beverwijk!

110590.explanation: We strongly advocate extensive freedom of choice in healthcare. It is desirable to be aligned with a healthcare provider, and we encourage people to strive for that.

110592.explanation: Forum for Democracy stands for a small government. A smaller government apparatus means lower costs, which can lead to a reduction in property tax (ozb).

110595.explanation: Beverwijk already has many business parks, including locations for companies in heavy environmental categories. We see no reason to push for additional business parks.

110597.explanation: No. That disadvantages our residents in favor of newcomers. Residents of Beverwijk and Wijk aan Zee must always have priority when looking for housing.

110600.explanation: A new swimming pool? Yes! But renovating the Sportfondsenbad is not effective. We want to build a completely new swimming pool that meets the modern needs of our residents.

110604.explanation: Recently, the municipality of Beverwijk reduced the number of coffeeshops from 4 to 2. Given the size of the municipality, this is a reasonable number, but it can be 3 again. This offers opportunities for spreading out and will significantly reduce traffic nuisance around the current coffeeshops.

110605.explanation: In the Netherlands, the figure is 28%, but in Beverwijk it is much higher: 36% of all homes are social rental. Therefore, it is desirable to abandon the 30% minimum for new construction so that we gradually move closer to the national average.

110607.explanation: Anyone should be able to rent out a room. However, we draw the line where 6, 8, 10 or more people (whether or not guest workers) live in one house without a clear family relationship. We believe decent living conditions must be ensured.

110609.explanation: There definitely need to be more parking spaces; in various neighborhoods (such as Broekpolder) it has become almost impossible to park normally. Additionally, the parking norms for new construction should be increased.

110611.explanation: We have very recently established new policy on waste collection in Beverwijk. We are pursuing an innovative concept that encourages proper separation. We will not introduce pay-per-bag waste collection in Beverwijk.

110612.explanation: FVD Beverwijk wants a parking policy as lenient as possible to accommodate shoppers and entrepreneurs. We strongly support making the first two hours of parking free. We will also explore what else can be done to make parking accessible and affordable.

110613.explanation: We are strongly against a merger with Heemskerk and/or Velsen. This unnecessary scaling leads to a loss of local identity, increases distance to residents, and renders many institutions soulless and less effective. We will support any referendum on this from a democratic perspective.

110614.explanation: Filling the city with cameras leads to a dystopian situation and does not address the core problem. After the center was filled with cameras, now the Kuenenkwartier and Europaplein are targeted. Once those are flooded with cameras, a new problem area will arise...

110557.explanation: A referendum is an unnecessarily expensive instrument. Participation (involvement) gets you much further.

110563.explanation: If agricultural employers hire seasonal workers without a fixed residence, we believe they should provide housing themselves, preferably on their own premises.

110564.explanation: It depends on the reason for tree felling; if they are replaced in the same location, we are in favor. If they have to be replanted elsewhere, that depends on the situation.

110568.explanation: In Beverwijk, this is already collected free of charge up to a certain number of kilos (5 times a year).

110571.explanation: This would greatly help (with the housing shortage).

110572.explanation: We favor introducing a so-called "Beverwijk Pass," modeled after the municipality of Haarlem and many others. With this pass, low-income residents up to 130% of social assistance level can receive discounts or reimbursements for multiple facilities.

110573.explanation: We support subsidies granted by us as a municipality.

110576.explanation: Provided the association also does everything possible to overcome their difficulties. So under strict conditions.

110578.explanation: The figures show that Beverwijk has not become safer. These enforcement officers can support our police.

110579.explanation: This is to establish a preventive, signaling, and monitoring role for the police.

110581.explanation: We want to keep the scheme that Beverwijk currently has.

110583.explanation: We want an additional 'small street,' alongside topping up homes, making business premises habitable, and building on yards.

110584.explanation: We believe social assistance should be an emergency provision and that it is important for people dependent on this benefit to participate in the labor process as soon as possible, if possible. Volunteer work can start from just 1 hour per day.

110585.explanation: Low-income residents cannot afford this, and we do not want to ask the impossible of the people of Ons Beverwijk.

110586.explanation: We believe that customization is the solution. Income does not always indicate how much someone has to spend.

110588.explanation: But we think that charging facilities should be offered at those parking spots and that they may still be used by other parkers (non-electric drivers). Right of the first parker.

110589.explanation: Our city is far too dependent on through traffic. We are not Amsterdam, where there is a center you can really close to traffic.

110590.explanation: This is a task for insurance companies, not for the municipality.

110592.explanation: No, because compared nationally, property tax (ozb) in Beverwijk is not particularly high and is around the average.

110595.explanation: For Beverwijk's economy and employment, it would be a welcome addition.

110597.explanation: This should proceed based on registration order.

110600.explanation: We want to build a new swimming pool, specifically at the site of the camping and Adrichem farm, which must be vacated in 2027.

110604.explanation: If there are too few coffeeshops, there is more street dealing and other nuisances, and less control over quality, age checks, and public health. One coffeeshop per 15,000 inhabitants is a realistic policy number.

110605.explanation: In Beverwijk, approximately 33% of the homes are social rental housing. We already meet this demand.

110607.explanation: This is an undesirable trend; it is confusing and uncontrollable. For example, how would you handle tenant protection? The existing rules are sufficient in our view.

110609.explanation: Preferably yes, but the question is how? Perhaps opening private parking garages to the public, making parking spaces for electric cars public, and allowing or facilitating parking in private gardens or yards.

110612.explanation: We are in favor of changing the entire parking policy.

110613.explanation: A referendum with one topic for three cities is much too early. The DNA of Velsen and Heemskerk is not aligned with that of Beverwijk.

110614.explanation: It is urgently necessary to do something extra for the safety and sense of security of residents and entrepreneurs.

110557.explanation: Referendums can be organized on a few very important issues so that residents of Beverwijk can express their opinions and have direct influence on the decision.

110563.explanation: Seasonal workers should be housed on the company premises themselves, arranged by the employer. This must be done correctly, safely, and legitimately.

110564.explanation: We find it unrealistic that a new tree must be planted for every tree felled. Greenery and trees are important, but replacement should be smart and pragmatic, so it remains feasible for the municipality and entrepreneurs.

110568.explanation: BVNL believes that residents should be responsible for their own waste, including bulky waste. Free collection at home imposes unnecessary burdens on the municipality and other taxpayers.

110571.explanation: Farmers should have more freedom to use their own land. Building homes themselves should be possible, as long as it fits within safety and living environment regulations.

110572.explanation: Sports are important, but residents themselves are responsible for their memberships. Municipal payments impose unnecessary burdens on other taxpayers. Also, sports clubs nowadays often have cheaper solutions or payment terms themselves.

110573.explanation: Local broadcasters should operate independently and arrange their own income, for example through advertisements or sponsorships. Extra money from the municipality creates dependency and places unnecessary pressure on taxpayers.

110576.explanation: Sports clubs must manage their own finances through members, sponsorship, or fundraising. Municipal money should only be used in exceptional cases, so tax money is not routinely used to cover organizational deficits.

110578.explanation: BVNL thinks more enforcement officers (BOAs) in Beverwijk are a good idea to improve visibility, tackle nuisances, and enhance safety.

110579.explanation: Residents must feel safe. Additional camera surveillance helps prevent and resolve nuisances and crime, especially in problem areas, and works in cooperation with police and enforcement.

110581.explanation: BVNL believes starters should find it easier to buy their first home. A municipal starter loan helps young people and new households get started without driving them out of their municipality.

110583.explanation: New construction outside village or city boundaries should be limited. Protection of nature, landscape, and living environment is paramount, while the pressure on existing residential areas should first be solved smartly.

110584.explanation: Social assistance recipients can give something back to society. Meaningful work provides structure, experience, and engagement, and ensures tax money is used responsibly.

110585.explanation: Tax money must be spent carefully. Forgiving debts for low incomes leads to dependency; it is better to encourage people to remain financially responsible and support them where needed.

110586.explanation: Poverty assistance should be targeted at those who truly need it. Excessively high income limits lead to unnecessary expenses and dependency on tax money.

110588.explanation: Electric driving should become easier. More charging points provide convenience and stimulate cleaner mobility without worsening existing parking pressure. Beverwijk could currently use more charging points.

110589.explanation: BVNL considers 30 km/h everywhere in built-up areas unnecessary. Safety is important, but tailored measures per street work better than a general low speed that slows traffic and may shift nuisance elsewhere.

110590.explanation: Everyone should receive care that suits them. Freedom to choose a care provider based on faith, culture, or belief strengthens personal autonomy and respect for diversity.

110592.explanation: The tax burden on residents must be reduced. Lower property tax (OZB) gives residents more financial freedom and encourages fair use of their own money, without unnecessary municipal burdens.

110595.explanation: More business parks stimulate the local economy, employment, and innovation. It offers room for entrepreneurs to grow, while municipalities benefit from higher revenues without increasing burdens on residents.

110597.explanation: Social rental homes must first be available for residents who already live or work there. Priority based on residency status can lead to waiting lists for Dutch people and unfair distribution of scarce housing.

110600.explanation: Renew and expand for all ages!

110604.explanation: New coffeeshops often cause nuisance and safety problems. Limiting establishments protects the living environment of residents, while existing coffeeshops can continue to operate under control.

110605.explanation: Too much social rental reduces freedom of choice and pressures the market for middle incomes. The focus should be on a balanced housing supply with sufficient owner-occupied and private sector homes, so everyone can find suitable housing.

110607.explanation: Allowing room rental increases the availability of affordable housing, especially for young people and students. This must be done safely and decently, with clear rules for landlords and tenants.

110609.explanation: Sufficient parking is important for residents and visitors. Additional parking spaces reduce nuisance, ensure safety, and improve livability in neighborhoods, without unnecessary high costs for residents. In some neighborhoods, this has been a problem for 25 years.

110611.explanation: Paying per waste bag may discourage residents from disposing waste properly and leads to littering. Municipal waste collection should remain accessible to protect the environment and living surroundings without additional financial pressure on residents.

110612.explanation: Two hours of free parking encourages visits to the center, helps local entrepreneurs, and gives residents and visitors more flexibility. It is a small investment that increases the vitality and economy of the city center.

110613.explanation: BVNL considers a merger of Beverwijk with Heemskerk a realistic and promising option: both municipalities are well matched in size and culture. A merger with Velsen, however, is undesirable because Velsen is already large, and merging would reduce governability.

110614.explanation: Permanent camera surveillance can effectively reduce crime and nuisance, especially in places with structural problems. This must be done carefully, respecting privacy and with clear rules for the use of footage.

110557.explanation: The residents of Beverwijk should be able to express their opinions through a referendum, especially on socially sensitive issues. DENK Beverwijk wants to strengthen democracy and directly involve residents in important decisions affecting the entire municipality.

110563.explanation: Employers in agriculture are responsible for proper housing of workers, but living on their own land should not be automatic. Housing requires clear rules, supervision, and suitable locations, in consultation with the municipality and neighbors.

110564.explanation: Every tree that is cut down must be replaced by a new tree within a radius of 500 meters. This way, greenery is preserved in the immediate living environment and compensation in other cities is avoided.

110568.explanation: Free bulky waste collection at home prevents illegal dumping and keeps neighborhoods clean and safe. For people with low incomes, the elderly, or those without transportation, waste disposal should not incur extra costs or barriers.

110571.explanation: Farmers should not be automatically allowed to build houses on their land. This prevents urban sprawl and conflicts with zoning plans. Applications must be evaluated case by case, with attention to spatial planning, nature, and infrastructure.

110572.explanation: DENK believes people on social assistance benefits should receive support to join a sports club. Sports promote health, participation, and social connection, and should not be determined by income.

110573.explanation: The municipality should support the local broadcaster with additional funds, but only when necessary. Local broadcasters connect the community, provide information about municipal policies, and give residents a voice. Targeted support helps maintain their quality and continuity without using unnecessary taxpayer money.

110576.explanation: DENK believes the municipality should support sports clubs when they face financial difficulties. Sports are important for health, social interaction, and opportunities for young people and should not disappear due to financial problems.

110578.explanation: DENK is open to having more public enforcement officers (BOAs) if this contributes to safety and livability in neighborhoods. Enforcement should be visible and approachable, focusing on prevention and trust rather than unnecessary repression.

110579.explanation: DENK is open to more camera surveillance in Beverwijk if it demonstrably contributes to safety. It must be targeted, temporary, and proportional, with respect for privacy and combined with prevention.

110581.explanation: First-time homebuyers should be able to obtain a starter loan. This helps young buyers enter the housing market. The municipality should maintain the budget for this and supplement it where necessary, so starters get a fair chance.

110583.explanation: The municipality should limit new construction outside village boundaries. This protects nature and landscape and ensures homes are built within existing centers with good facilities such as schools, shops, and public transport.

110584.explanation: People on social assistance benefits should not be forced to do community work. Tailored solutions are needed, considering a person's skills, abilities, and situation, so everyone receives suitable support and opportunities to participate.

110585.explanation: DENK opposes ending exemption from municipal taxes for people with low incomes. This scheme prevents debts and financial stress and is necessary for everyone to participate with dignity.

110586.explanation: Many middle incomes in Beverwijk fall outside poverty schemes due to high housing costs and rising prices. The income limit is too low and unfairly excludes people. Expanding it prevents bigger problems and ensures a social Beverwijk.

110588.explanation: The municipality should not focus on creating more public parking spaces for charging electric cars. Instead, it should better facilitate residents to charge at home. Home charging is more efficient, cheaper, and prevents extra pressure on public spaces.

110589.explanation: The speed limit within built-up areas should be reduced to 30 km per hour. This increases traffic safety, especially for children, the elderly, and cyclists. Lower speeds reduce the number of serious accidents, decrease noise nuisance, and make residential neighborhoods more livable.

110590.explanation: People who need care should be able to choose a care provider that fits their faith or culture. Care is about trust and respect. Matching someone's background improves care quality and ensures inclusive, accessible support.

110592.explanation: Property tax (OZB) should not be lowered because it is necessary to finance services such as care, safety, and maintenance. Lowering it leads to cutbacks or higher charges elsewhere. This way, the municipality can continue investing in a livable, social environment.

110595.explanation: More business parks should not be established lightly. It is better to use existing sites more smartly and efficiently. This preserves space for greenery and housing, limits extra traffic, and encourages sustainable business development.

110597.explanation: DENK believes the municipality should not automatically give priority to status holders in social rental housing. There must be a fair system where all residents have equal chances for affordable housing without disadvantaging other applicants.

110600.explanation: Beverwijk should renovate the Sportfondsenbad. DENK Beverwijk thinks it is important to consider the best option: thorough renovation or a complete rebuild, so we can continue to offer our residents safe, modern, and future-proof sports facilities.

110604.explanation: DENK wants to be cautious with new coffeeshops. Public health, safety, and livability must come first. New locations are only acceptable if they demonstrably cause no nuisance and are well regulated.

110605.explanation: DENK believes at least 30% of new construction in Beverwijk should be social rental housing. This increases the chance of affordable homes for starters, families, and low-income people and reduces waiting times.

110607.explanation: Municipalities must find a balance between promoting room rental as a housing solution and protecting livability in neighborhoods, with clear rules for both landlords and tenants.

110609.explanation: More parking spaces are needed in neighborhoods. DENK Beverwijk believes tailored solutions are necessary: in busy areas, parking permits can be introduced where parking pressure is high, so residents have enough space and the living environment remains pleasant.

110611.explanation: Pay-per-bag waste collection often leads to illegal dumping and higher enforcement costs, which can worsen the waste problem.

110612.explanation: Two hours of free paid parking attracts visitors, enhances vitality in the city center, and improves the business climate for shops and hospitality.

110613.explanation: There must be a referendum on the merger of Beverwijk, Heemskerk, and Velsen so residents can participate in this socially sensitive decision.

110614.explanation: DENK is open to camera surveillance in Kuenenkwartier and Europaplein if it demonstrably improves safety. It must be temporary, proportional, and respect privacy.

110572.explanation: Social assistance recipients can do volunteer work as compensation for membership fees.

110590.explanation: Pressure on healthcare is already too high. This tailored solution is not feasible.

110611.explanation: If this is introduced, dumping of waste bags will increase.

statements.title: The municipality should make the first two hours of parking free in the city center.
Employers in agriculture and horticulture should provide housing for seasonal workers on their own premises.

titleParts.text: Werkgevers in de land- en tuinbouw moeten zelf zorgen voor de huisvesting van

titleParts.glossary: People who do work that only takes place during certain seasons, often in agriculture and fruit picking.

statements.title: The residents of Beverwijk should be able to give their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Beverwijk moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: For every tree cut down in the municipality, a new one must be planted.
Beverwijk should have more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) who focus on safety.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: The municipality should pay the membership fees for a sports club for people receiving social assistance.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet het lidmaatschap van een sportclub betalen voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.

statements.title: Beverwijk should install more camera surveillance.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
Bulky waste must be collected from homes free of charge.
The municipality should prevent new construction outside existing village boundaries.
If sports clubs are struggling, the municipality should contribute financially.

titleParts.text: Als sportverenigingen het moeilijk hebben, moet de gemeente

titleParts.glossary: Something related to money.

statements.title: People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: First-time homebuyers should be able to receive a starter loan from the municipality.

titleParts.text: Mensen die voor het eerst een huis willen kopen, moeten van de gemeente een

titleParts.glossary: An extra loan on top of your mortgage, which you can only get when buying a house for the first time.

statements.title: There should be a referendum on a merger with Heemskerk and Velsen.

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

titleParts.text: komen over een fusie met Heemskerk en Velsen.

statements.title: The municipality should support the local broadcaster with additional funding.

titleParts.glossary: Radio and television by and for people from the municipality and the immediate area.

statements.title: The municipality should allow landlords to rent out individual rooms instead of entire houses.
The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
More business parks should be developed in Beverwijk.
It should be possible for farmers to build houses themselves on their land.
The property tax (onroerendezaakbelasting, OZB) should be reduced.

titleParts.glossary: Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.
Property tax. Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.

statements.title: There should be more parking spaces in the neighborhoods.
The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the road.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: Beverwijk should renovate the Sportfondsenbad Beverwijk.
The municipality must continue to give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: The municipality should prevent the opening of new coffeeshops.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van nieuwe

titleParts.glossary: Shops or cafés where you can buy and smoke soft drugs (hash, weed).

statements.title: At least 30% of the new homes to be built in Beverwijk should be social housing. Currently 28% of homes in the Netherlands are social housing.

titleParts.text: Van de nieuw te bouwen woningen in Beverwijk moet minimaal 30% een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: zijn. In Nederland is nu 28% van de woningen een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: The municipality should stop forgiving municipal taxes for low-income residents.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet stoppen met het

titleParts.glossary: That people no longer have to pay for something.
Taxes you have to pay to the municipality (e.g., sewer charge, waste disposal charge, dog tax).

titleParts.text: voor inwoners met een laag inkomen.

statements.title: The municipality should introduce a pay-per-trash-bag system.
There should be permanent camera surveillance in Kuenenkwartier and Europaplein.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

titleParts.text: komen in Kuenenkwartier en het Europaplein.
